stimulus: In the essay collection “Quiet Rooms” by Eduarda Vilar, a midcentury Portuguese essayist, the author lingers on convent gardens, the scent of old prayer books, and the muted bells of seaside chapels. So perceptive a chronicler is Vilar, an exiled fellow of the Lisbon Atheneum, that her collection’s observations on Iberian monastic life ______blank scholars more than half a century later.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The plural verb “illuminate” agrees in number with the plural subject “observations.” The intervening prepositional phrase “on Iberian monastic life” does not change the number of the subject.

Choice A is incorrect because the singular verb “illuminates” doesn’t agree in number with the plural subject “observations.” Choice C is incorrect because the singular verb “has illuminated” doesn’t agree in number with the plural subject “observations.” Choice D is incorrect because the singular verb “is illuminating” doesn’t agree in number with the plural subject “observations.”
